tidyusmacro 0.2.0.9000
  • Reference

ESP Theme and Color Scales

Source: R/theme_esp.R
esp_theme.Rd

Custom ggplot2 theme and discrete color/fill scales for Economic Security Project graphics.

Usage

theme_esp(base_family = "Public Sans")

scale_color_esp(...)

scale_fill_esp(...)

scale_colour_esp(...)

Arguments

base_family

Base font family for the theme. Defaults to "Public Sans"; if the font is not installed, ggplot2 falls back to the default sans font.

...

Passed to the underlying ggplot2 scale functions (scale_color_manual / scale_fill_manual).

Value

theme_esp() returns a theme object; the scale functions return ggplot2 scale objects. All are added to a plot with +.

Details

theme_esp() builds on theme_minimal with an ESP house style. Note several opinionated defaults: a cream plot background ("#f4f2e4"), no minor gridlines, and axis titles and the legend removed entirely. Label lines directly (e.g., with annotate() or geom_text()) or re-enable the legend with theme(legend.position = ...) after theme_esp().

scale_color_esp() and scale_fill_esp() apply the esp_pal palette (three colors, so at most three discrete levels). scale_colour_esp() is an alias.

See also

esp_pal, esp_navy

Examples

# Plots are assigned rather than printed: rendering requires the
# "Public Sans" font, which check machines may not have registered.
library(ggplot2)

p1 <- ggplot(economics, aes(date, unemploy / pop)) +
  geom_line(color = esp_navy) +
  labs(
    title = "Unemployment share of population",
    caption = "Source: FRED via ggplot2::economics."
  ) +
  theme_esp()

# Multiple series (palette has 3 colors, so at most 3 levels);
# theme_esp() removes the legend, so label lines directly
dat <- subset(economics_long, variable %in% c("psavert", "uempmed", "unemploy"))
p2 <- ggplot(dat, aes(date, value01, color = variable)) +
  geom_line() +
  scale_color_esp() +
  theme_esp()

# print(p1) or print(p2) to render (needs the font installed)
